  1. Home
  2. rule
  3. Asia
  4. India

India (Bharat)

How to Distinguish

  • The domain is .in(fixed 2023/7/31, thanks for the comment)
  • There are many languages such as Hindi, and they vary by region
  • Distinctive utility poles are common (see Indian poles)

Signs you can find

Representative Companies

    The language is distinctive and sometimes only English is used. In city centers, addresses are often written in English below signs, so identifying the city can be quick. However, India’s address system is not fully standardized, and packages sometimes fail to arrive(References India EC market report - JETRO (JP)), so even with an address, narrowing down the exact road can still be hard.

    Distinctive pole shapes that are rare elsewhere (see Indian poles). The most common type has three branches at the top, resembling a mountain or an anchor. The video below is an interview during recent floods, and you can see poles in the background.

    Wiring on poles is often messy.

    Image quality is often poor with heavy blur. Other countries can also be blurry, so don’t rely on image quality alone.

    Cambodia Sri Lanka Bangladesh
    Nigeria Lebanon Ecuador
    Finland

    There are bollards similar to Cambodia. There are also black-and-white bollard-like posts.

    Plates come in several colors: white, yellow, black, etc. Some look like EU plates, so be careful(References Different Types of Number Plates in India, their Meaning, Usages and More).

    Self-made; approximate shape, not exact.

    {{< amazoncard url="https://amzn.to/4iIaGQR" title="Northeast India in 45 Chapters (Area Studies)" price="¥1,760" tagline="Northeast India, made up of eight states including Assam, has distinct ethnic, cultural, and religious features and has become a development focus in recent years. This book covers everything from geography to politics and explores the region’s diversity and complex history." >}}

    Narrow down state/region

    Languages

    • Each state has a different flag
    • Languages differ by region(by usumdensetu )

      #India's 22 scheduled #languages by the name of the script they use pic.twitter.com/PBDqehiogD

      — India in Pixels by Ashris (@indiainpixels) January 17, 2021

      インドの主要な言語の分布と、その見分け方について解説しました！#GeoGuessr pic.twitter.com/NL4wmCBzcz

      — LAKE TOUYA / とうや (@geotouya) November 5, 2023

      Around Ahmedabad. Stepwells are also common around Ahmedabad.

      By Aksi great, Dipan Shukla - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link

      You can often spot a triangular ▷ shape in the script.

      Many speakers in Karnataka and around Bengaluru(References Kannada).

      Hindi/Hindustani is a natural language spoken in regions that include Delhi.

      Common along the Sri Lanka coastline.

      Telugu (తెలుగు) is the official language of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The letters often contain shapes like “tsu”, ”✓”, or “m” around the characters(References Telugu).

      Used in Kerala and Lakshadweep(References Malayalam).

      Found in limited Tibetan cultural areas such as Leh in Ladakh.

      CC0, Link

      Vehicles

      • State abbreviations appear on license plates
      • Rickshaw colors may vary by region
        • Yellow around the windows, green below: seen nationwide but more common in the north(from 『Tips and Metas for Geoguessr's NEWEST Country!! (feat. Duskden2)』)
        • Yellow around the windows, yellow below: try the south?

        State abbreviations are written on plates. They can often be seen on the sides of buses or trucks, but image quality is sometimes too poor to read. See List of Indian states for details.

        State Abbrev. Example
        Mizoram MZ
        Himachal Pradesh HP
        West Bengal WB
        Kerala KL
        Tamil Nadu TN

        By Maximilian Dörrbecker (Chumwa) - Own work, CC BY-SA 2.5, Link

        Seen nationwide, but more common in the north(from 『Tips and Metas for Geoguessr’s NEWEST Country!! (feat. Duskden2)』)

        If it is yellow, try the south?

        Architecture

        • There are many houses with brick-like walls or concrete walls and flat tiles, but this is not certain

          There are many houses with red-painted walls or concrete walls with flat tiles, but this is uncertain.

          The old city of Jodhpur has many blue-painted walls, and blue buildings can be spotted(References Jodhpur). But they also appear in the south, so it is hard to localize.

          Architecture 2

          • Goa: highest rainfall; tall palm trees
          • Kerala: tall palms like Goa, but more two-story buildings(References Traditional architecture in India and Nepal (JP))
          • Assam: earthquake and flood region; stilt houses and light-material walls appear

            The west coast has the highest rainfall, so steep roofs are used. Some houses appear to have a second roof on top. As a former part of Portuguese India, Western-style buildings can appear.

            Andaman Islands can feel similar to Goa or Assam, so be careful not to rule them out

            By Bharath maraboina123 - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

            Like Goa, it is rainy and has tall palm trees. Traditional Kerala architecture often uses two-story buildings(References Traditional architecture in India and Nepal (JP)).

            In high-rainfall regions like Kerala or Assam, tall trees like these are common(References Areca catechu).

            Assam is prone to earthquakes and floods, so light materials like bamboo and stilt houses may appear(References Comparative study on bungalow industrialization and reproduction (JP)). Heavy materials like brick can fail in earthquakes.

            By Nalbarian - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

            Pole shapes

            • Pole shapes differ by state or city
              • If there is a square hole near the top, it may be a Panjab city
              • A yellow rectangular sticker might indicate Uttar Pradesh(from 『THE INDIA GEOGUESSR GUIDE』)
              • Poles with holes may indicate Gujarat(from 『Tips and Metas for Geoguessr's NEWEST Country!! (feat. Duskden2) - zi8gzag』)
              • Sectioned poles may indicate Telangana/Andhra Pradesh?
              • A downward right triangle may indicate Madhya Pradesh?
              • Poles shaped like "キ" or "干" appear in Goa

              A square hole near the top suggests a Panjab city

              Yellow rectangular stickers can appear(from 『THE INDIA GEOGUESSR GUIDE』)? They may be torn or covered.

              Poles with holes (holy poles) appear(from 『Tips and Metas for Geoguessr’s NEWEST Country!! (feat. Duskden2) - zi8gzag』).

              Sectioned poles appear around Hyderabad?

              This mountain-shaped pole seems common everywhere, but a downward triangle at the bottom might be a clue.

              Poles shaped like “キ” or “干” feel very reliable.

              Soil color

              • Soil color varies by region, but image quality is poor and there are many exceptions, so combine with other hints
                • Red: reddish soil suggests southeast or south, but the range is broad
                • Black soil: also called regur soil; common around the Deccan Plateau, though not always black
                • Alluvial: light, sandy alluvium deposited by the Indus and Ganges in the north
                • Forest: Nepal’s east and west are forested

                Reddish soil: southeast; dark soil: inland south of Delhi; light sandy soil: northern image?

                By National Council of Educational Research and Training(NCERT), CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons

                Reddish soil suggests southeast or south, but the range is broad.

                Black soil is also called regur soil and is suitable for cotton(References Major soil deposits of India).

                Alluvial deposits from the Indus and Ganges are found in the north. They are generally flat with wetlands and consist of unconsolidated mud, sand, and stone(References Alluvium (JP)).

                Even with red soil, if the area is mountainous and heavily forested, consider Nepal’s east/west as well.

                Narrow down city/town

                • There is a city called Leh at over 3,600m where Tibetans form the majority
                • If you see yellow labels with numbers on street poles, it may be Chandigarh(from 『THE INDIA GEOGUESSR GUIDE』)

                  Tibetan script appears in town, and mountains are visible in all directions. Outside summer, access is only by air, and the town has an airport.

                  Representative companies

                  Company Code Description Earnings Dividend history
                  HDFC Bank NASDAQ India’s largest private bank by assets and market cap. Dividend History
                  ICICI Bank NASDAQ One of India’s largest banks; designated as a systemically important financial institution by the Reserve Bank of India(References Systemically important financial institution). Dividend History
                  Reliance Industries - India’s largest private company. A conglomerate spanning resources, petrochemicals, entertainment, and telecom. Member of the NIFTY 50(References NIFTY 50). - -
